sanskrit: ततः श्वेतैर् हयैर् युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ। माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः॥
transliteration: tataḥ śvetair hayair yukte mahati syandane sthitau mādhavaḥ pāṇḍavaś caiva divyau śaṅkhau pradadhmatuḥ
literal_meaning: Then, seated on a great chariot yoked with white horses, Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa) and the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna) blew their divine conch-shells.
contextual_meaning: At the precise moment when the battlefield is charged with dread, Arjuna and Kṛṣṇa respond not with hesitation but with a resonant declaration of presence. Their conch-blast is a psychological anchor: it externalizes inner resolve, transforms anxiety into focused intention, and signals that the Self (Kṛṣṇa) and ego (Arjuna) are now aligned. The white horses symbolize purified life-energy ready to move forward, while the divine conches imply that the ensuing action is not merely personal but cosmically supported.
psychological_state: A person who has just moved from paralysis to decisive action by internally integrating a higher purpose with personal agency.
trigger_scenario: A team leader who must announce a painful restructuring, feeling the weight of livelihoods at stake, suddenly senses clarity and support from a trusted mentor beside them, and proceeds with steady voice.
anchor_text: Sound your truth; let purified will and Self unite to move life forward.
